archimate:BusinessObject.name: Blockchain policy
archimate:BusinessObject.documentation:
Definition: A Blockchain policy, as a specialization of a Data policy, can be defined as a set of rules, guidelines, and controls that govern the management, access, and utilization of data within a blockchain network or ecosystem. It outlines the specific protocols, procedures, and requirements for storing, validating, and securing data on the blockchain, ensuring integrity, immutability, and transparency.
Source: OECD
Source reference: https://www.oecd.org/daf/blockchain/
LOST view: SV-Governance Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/BlockchainPolicyBusinessObject
ABB name: egovera:BlockchainPolicyBusinessObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/BlockchainPolicyBusinessObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataPolicy

properties.value: A Blockchain policy, as a specialization of a Data policy, can be defined as a set of rules, guidelines, and controls that govern the management, access, and utilization of data within a blockchain network or ecosystem. It outlines the specific protocols, procedures, and requirements for storing, validating, and securing data on the blockchain, ensuring integrity, immutability, and transparency.
